prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the part that does all the thinking and figuring things out. When you click on something or type a message, the CPU is the one that gets busy making it happen. It doesn’t store your pictures or songs—that’s what the memory and hard drive are for—but it’s the clever little helper that runs everything. Think of it like the conductor of an orchestra: all the other parts play their notes, but the CPU makes sure they’re all in tune and on time!
